meta_title: For Founders: email automation to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Email Automation to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Break free from tutorial hell with this expert checklist. Learn how to implement scalable email automation using Antigravity and Claude Code to drive revenue.
content.answer_block: Stop watching tutorials and start deploying. By integrating automated email triggers directly into your product architecture, you move from passive learning to active revenue generation. Use Antigravity to structure your backend logic and Claude Code to execute the implementation, focusing strictly on high-value, conversion-oriented user touchpoints.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks in the trenches of tutorial hell, collecting knowledge like digital hoarding, while your product remains a ghost in your IDE. The dopamine hit of finishing another video is a trap; it is a distraction from the only metric that matters: your MRR. Real founders don't watch; they build, break, and ship.

Today, we pivot to offensive engineering. We are implementing a robust email automation engine that turns passive users into paying customers. Using Antigravity to architect your flows and Claude Code to bridge the gap between intent and production, we are shifting your focus from 'learning' to 'launching.'

steps.title: Define High-Conversion Triggers
steps.description: Identify the specific user actions—like sign-up or feature completion—that correlate with revenue. We map these to automated email sequences.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Architect a modular event-driven email trigger system that listens for user lifecycle events and maps them to specific email templates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Create a trigger-based event handler in our backend to capture user signup and feature completion events.'

steps.title: Integrate Email Provider API
steps.description: Connect your backend to a delivery service like Resend or Postmark to ensure high deliverability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust service layer for a transactional email provider, including error handling and retry logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Install resend package and create an email service module with error handling.'

steps.title: Implement Dynamic Template Injection
steps.description: Move away from hardcoded messages. Use dynamic placeholders to personalize user emails at scale.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a template engine that consumes JSON payloads to populate personalized email content dynamically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Refactor current email templates to accept dynamic user data using a template engine.'

steps.title: Automate Lifecycle Sequences
steps.description: Program the logic for drip campaigns that guide users toward the 'Aha!' moment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a scheduling logic that triggers follow-up emails based on user inactivity after the initial sign-up.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a cron job or background task to trigger a 24-hour follow-up email if the user has not completed onboarding.'

steps.title: Monitor Analytics and Conversion
steps.description: Attach tracking pixels or webhook listeners to verify that your emails are actually driving clicks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop a webhook endpoint to receive and store email open/click statistics for conversion analysis.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Build a webhook endpoint to log open and click events from our email service into the database.'

pro_tips: Always use a dedicated sub-domain for transactional emails to protect your main domain's sender reputation.
Implement 'soft-gate' emails—give away value for free, but make the next logical step require a login.
Don't build your own email queue; leverage managed infrastructure to avoid dealing with bounce handling and throttling.
A/B test your subject lines using a small subset of users before automating the sequence for your entire list.
Focus on the 'Aha!' moment; your first automated email should move the user one inch closer to the core value proposition of your SaaS.
content.next_steps: Once your automated sequences are live, analyze the conversion rate for each email. Optimize the copy of the lowest-performing email and repeat until your funnel conversion increases by at least 15%.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the sake of learning and start building for the sake of revenue. You have the tools, the architecture, and the roadmap. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
